items.title: From the Gulf War of the 1990s to the 2020 pandemic, global crises generally follow a predictable path

items.title: Meta and Google found liable in landmark social media addiction lawsuit
items.summary: The case was brought on by a 20-year-old woman who accused the tech companies of causing harm by deliberately designing addictive platforms which worsened her mental health.

items.title: Trial of two men accused of raping woman after night out hears closing arguments
items.summary: The jury in the trial of two men accused of picking up a young woman waiting for an Uber after a night out and raping her in her home has heard closing submissions.

items.title: Australian shares soar on Middle East ceasefire hopes and softer inflation figures
items.summary: The local share market has its best day in almost a year after reports the US is seeking a ceasefire with Iran buoyed investor sentiment, and monthly inflation was softer than expected.

items.title: NASA pivots to moon base, nuclear propulsion in expanded multi-billion-dollar program
items.summary: NASA has cancelled plans to deploy a space station in lunar orbit and will instead build a $28.7 billion base on the moon's surface.

items.title: Iranian visa holders temporarily barred from travelling to Australia
items.summary: Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke says the war in Iran increases the risk some Iranians may not be able to leave Australia when their visa expires.

items.title: Shepparton man spared jail after leaving child in cardboard box during car theft
items.summary: A 32-year-old man says he is "ashamed" of his actions after he left a child in a cardboard box in a regional Victorian car park while stealing the vehicle the toddler was in.

items.title: Questions over deal between AI company Firmus and Tasmania's power generator
items.summary: Questions remain about the deal struck to supply power to an AI company in Tasmania's north, not long after a request to supply another manufacturer was rejected.

items.title: Russia fires nearly 400 drones at Ukraine with signs its spring offensive has begun
items.summary: Russia's anticipated annual spring offensive appears to have started, with a major drone assault on civilian areas coinciding with multiple attempts to break through frontline Ukrainian defences.

items.title: South Australian Premier Peter Malinauskas announces new cabinet ministers in major reshuffle
items.summary: South Australian Premier Peter Malinauskas has unveiled his new-look second-term cabinet. Among the changes, Chris Picton has been moved out of the health portfolio and replaced by former education minister Blair Boyer.

items.title: Meta ordered to pay $538m in US trial over child exploitation, user safety claims
items.summary: The verdict marks the first time a jury has ruled on such claims against Meta, as the company faces a wave of lawsuits over how its platforms affect young people's mental health.

items.title: Shorter showers, reduced flights and no street lights among measures to address Asia's energy crisis
items.summary: Countries across Asia are moving to protect their energy supplies after Iran effectively blockaded the crucial Strait of Hormuz passage in response to the United States and Israel's attacks.

items.title: Democrats flip seat in Florida district home to Donald Trump's Mar-a-Lago resort
items.summary: Democrats celebrate the victory as the latest sign voters are turning against Donald Trump and Republicans ahead of November's midterm elections.

items.title: Wallabies flyhalf Tom Lynagh returns for Reds, Zac Lomax joins Western Force teammates
items.summary: The Queensland Reds are attempting to achieve their first Super Rugby win in Wellington since 1998, and their maiden win at a ground known as the Cake Tin.

items.title: Hong Kong police arrest bookstore owner and staff for allegedly selling Jimmy Lai biography, broadcaster reports
items.summary: Hong Kong police have arrested a bookstore owner and three shopkeepers for allegedly selling "seditious" publications.

items.title: OpenAI pulls plug on AI video app Sora that sparked deepfake concerns
items.summary: Sora was released in September by the company behind ChatGPT and soon went viral as a place to share short-form videos generated by artificial intelligence, but it also raised alarms.

items.title: Waqas Haider charged with murder over Monaro Highway crash that killed Bega valley woman
items.summary: A 40-year-old Canberra man has been charged with murder after a crash that killed a Bega Valley woman on the Monaro Highway in early March.

items.title: Danish election bruises Mette Frederiksen's Social Democrats amid fights over immigration and Greenland
items.summary: Danish Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen's Social Democrats look set to suffer the party's worst election outcome in more than a century.

items.title: Public servant Nadine Marie Smith allegedly helped intimate partner skip social housing waitlist
items.summary: A 55-year-old public servant is accused of illegally offering a social housing tenancy to her intimate partner, allowing them to skip a large number of applicants who were in the queue for a home.

items.title: Inflation cooled slightly in February before energy price rise caused by Iran war
items.summary: The latest monthly data does not capture the recent rise in energy prices caused by the war in the Middle East, which began on February 28.

items.title: 25yo man dies in NT police custody after arrest and detainment in police car cage in Darwin
items.summary: A 25-year-old Indigenous man has died in police custody in Darwin's northern suburbs overnight, with police saying he was "arrested without incident".

items.title: Government talks down fuel rationing, as Chalmers concedes 5 per cent inflation scenario 'conservative'
items.summary: The government has requested new Treasury modelling for "more challenging circumstances" stemming from the Middle East war, admitting that the economic scenarios released last week "look pretty conservative now".

items.title: Married at First Sight expert Mel Schilling remembered as 'amazing' and 'radiant' after bowel cancer death
items.summary: Mel Schilling became one of Australian TV's most recognised relationship experts, but who was she, and what led to her death?

items.title: Drone baiting aims to eradicate infestation of house mice from remote WA island
items.summary: Scientists are using "world-first" drone-baiting technology to target thousands of house mice in an effort to bring migratory seabirds back from the brink on Browse Island off Western Australia's north.

items.title: Jetstar reduces flights between New Zealand and Australia due to rising jet fuel prices
items.summary: Jetstar announces it is reducing flights between Australia and New Zealand due to the rising cost of jet fuel as a result of the war in the Middle East.

items.title: Man accused of raping woman in her apartment testifies 'she wasn't that drunk'
items.summary: Panormitis Charalampis, co-accused of picking up an intoxicated young woman from outside a nightclub while she was waiting for an uber and sexually assaulting her at her apartment, took the stand, undergoing hours of intense cross-examination.

items.title: Couples on sleeping separately and if it's helped — or hindered — their relationships
items.summary: Snoring, shift work, parenting and health — you shared the many reasons why you no longer sleep in the same bed as your partner, and whether it's been a positive move for sleep and the relationship.

items.title: Tom Gleeson, Aaron Chen, Paul McDermott and more on 40 years of the Melbourne Comedy Festival
items.summary: The very first Melbourne International Comedy Festival launched 40 years ago, and while it's kept its scrappy charm, things look pretty different today.

items.title: Katherine River could hit major flood level again as BOM warns of lingering ex-TC Narelle impacts
items.summary: Residents in Katherine are bracing for the possibility of major riverine flooding today, which would mark the second significant weather event to rock the town in a month.

items.title: Major facelift proposed for Mount Gambier's sunken Umpherston Sinkhole garden
items.summary: The Mount Gambier council is asking for feedback on a significant change to the regional city's beloved sunken garden.

items.title: WA Opposition Leader Basil Zempilas 'not fearful' of One Nation's rise ahead of 2029 WA election
items.summary: Basil Zempilas is "not fearful" of the soaring rise of Pauline Hanson's One Nation in the west, but acknowledges his WA Liberals will need to decide whether to direct preference votes to the controversial party or distance themselves.

items.title: Michael Atherton, Michael Vaughan and Jonny Bairstow question England's Ashes review
items.summary: Former England captains and current player Jonny Bairstow question the findings of an internal review into a disastrous Ashes campaign, which saw the team's leaders maintain their positions.

items.title: Federal and state governments announce $2b bailout for Rio Tinto's Boyne aluminium smelter
items.summary: Rio Tinto, the federal and state governments have struck a landmark partnership to secure the long-term future of the Boyne aluminium smelter.

items.title: Trump claims to have received 'very big present' from Iran
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Cacti confiscated from home garden over fears of another 'prickly pear' disaster
items.summary: An opal miner's garden plants have been destroyed after it was identified as a prohibited plant, potentially as invasive as one of Australia's most notorious plants.

items.title: Mo Salah announces he will leave Liverpool at end of EPL season
items.summary: Mo Salah announces he is leaving Liverpool after almost a decade of starring for the EPL giants.

items.title: Third man charged over the alleged abduction and murder of Chris Baghsarian
items.summary: Police allege the 23-year-old man was a "major player" in organising the abduction plot.

items.title: Football Australia vows high-quality Matildas opponents in lead-up to 2027 Women's World Cup
items.summary: After the highs of their run to the Asian Cup final, in just over two weeks the Matildas travel to Kenya to face teams ranked outside the top 100.

items.title: Alleged northern NSW bank robber arrested after 30 years on the run
items.summary: A man wanted by police over two bank robberies in NSW almost 30 years ago has been arrested and charged.

items.title: Location of Brisbane's Victoria Park stadium for 2032 Olympic and Paralympic Games unveiled
items.summary: New artist impressions showing the interior of the 63,000-seat arena have also been released.

items.title: Russia fires nearly 400 drones in deadly attack on Ukraine, with UNESCO site targeted
items.summary: Three were killed and 30 wounded in a rare daytime attack, including on the city of Lviv, while four have been killed in an overnight bombardment.

items.title: Tropical Cyclone Narelle re-forms off WA, expected to reach category four
items.summary: Having cut a swathe across northern Australia over the past week, Tropical Cyclone Narelle is now threatening coastal communities in Western Australia.

items.title: Queensland wood mulch company accuses Ipswich council of 'political agenda'
items.summary: Queensland's waste industry has blasted a local council's "political agenda" over a decision requiring an odorous wood mulching site at Ipswich to be covered up quickly.

items.title: Wine growers fear smoke taint will spoil Victorian harvest as planned burns begin
items.summary: Just months after devastating bushfires, wine growers are concerned planned burns in regional Victoria pose a new risk to their grapes.

items.title: E-bike changes coming to NSW include reduced power and proposed age limit
items.summary: The NSW government has recently announced staged reforms to improve safety for e-bike riders and pedestrians. This is what it'll mean for the community.

items.title: What you need to know about Iran today, with Matthew Doran
items.summary: The focus of the Middle East war shifts to Lebanon as Israel plans to occupy southern parts of the country.

items.title: First Western Sydney Airport international flight tickets go on sale
items.summary: Singapore Airlines put their first international flights from WSI on sale this morning, confirming the airline will be among the first to run from Sydney's new airport.

items.title: The Malinauskas government's second term could be harder than its first
items.summary: Despite holding an overwhelming majority in parliament, the Malinauskas government faces no shortage of policy and political challenges to navigate in its second term.

items.title: Donald Trump says Iran sent US 'big present' as Pentagon readies thousands more troops for Middle East
items.summary: The US president suggests a mystery gift "worth a tremendous amount" is aiding diplomatic efforts to end the war, and comes as he reportedly prepares to send a major airborne force to the region.

items.title: Waste collectors warn bin services may stop if diesel not found urgently
items.summary: Waste collectors say they are struggling to acquire diesel and many are operating at "unsustainable" losses, putting the collection of household bins and commercial waste under threat.

items.title: Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke admits luck — not a plan — saved lives in Perth Australia Day failed bomb attack
items.summary: The home affairs minister says Australia was "so lucky" to avoid mass casualties during the attempted Australia Day bomb attack in Perth and admits the nation was slow to recognise the gravity of the alleged terrorist act.

items.title: NDIS participants work more, feel better after accessing scheme, report finds
items.summary: New analysis by the e61 Institute, which tracks NDIS participants over more than a decade, finds substantial improvements to their workforce participation and self-reported energy levels within four years of accessing the taxpayer-funded scheme.

items.title: Did AI really take their jobs?
items.summary: As big tech companies sack staff and say AI will pick up the slack, workers are left to grapple with what's really going on.

items.title: The Kates, Kate McLennan and Kate McCartney, take crime comedy Deadloch to Darwin
items.summary: Kate McLennan and Kate McCartney (Get Krack!n) set out to make a "funny Broadchurch" with crime comedy Deadloch. Now, with fans around the world, it's back for season two.

items.title: Travel quiz, March 25: Do you have the stamina to complete this trip?
items.summary: Which country has the dong as its currency? And where would you dine on tteokbokki?

items.title: Married at First Sight expert Mel Schilling dies, aged 54
items.summary: Schilling's husband, Gareth Brisbane, took to social media on Tuesday night to reveal the news.

items.title: Trump Organization paid for Gold Coast Mayor Tom Tate's meals and more during Mar-a-lago stay
items.summary: The Gold Coast mayor visited the luxurious estate days before an Australian developer signed a deal to build a Trump-branded tower at Surfers Paradise.

items.title: The computer program deciding the level of care for elderly Australians
items.summary: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

items.title: Former defence leaders outline already-present fossil fuel dependence, climate disinformation threats
items.summary: Australians are already deeply emersed in a global disinformation war about fossil fuels and climate change, former defence leaders say in an extensive new review.

items.title: Gold Coast Suns's Bailey Humphrey fails at AFL tribunal to get dangerous tackle ban overturned
items.summary: Young gun Bailey Humphrey will miss Gold Coast's next two matches through suspension, after the AFL tribunal rejects his arguments against a rough conduct ban.

items.summary: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

items.title: Accused Easey Street killer Perry Kouroumblis to face rape charge
items.summary: Prosecutors have reinstated a rape charge against accused Easey Street killer Perry Kouroumblis after a magistrate had earlier struck out the offence.

items.title: Katherine bracing for possible further major flood as river set to reach 17.5m
items.summary: Exhausted residents in the Northern Territory town of Katherine are once again on alert as they prepare for further possible major flooding on Wednesday.

items.title: Brisbane Broncos 'surprised' as Ben Te'o resigns after dispute with coach Michael Maguire
items.summary: Brisbane defensive coach Ben Te'o, the mastermind behind the scrambling defence in the 2025 grand final win, hands in his resignation.

items.title: Tropical Cyclone Narelle set to impact Perth over weekend, with heavy rainfall expected
items.summary: Tropical Cyclone Narelle is set to impact Perth and towns north of the city over the weekend with heavy rainfall expected.
